title: dryopteris papuana
intro: Dryopteris papuana, also known as the New Guinea shield fern, is a species of fern native to New Guinea and surrounding islands. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves of the Dryopteris papuana contain flavonoids and other compounds that offer anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing benefits for cardiovascular health, immune system function, and overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its potential medicinal properties to be utilized for therapeutic purposes.
